texto: I look at the human sciences as poetic sciences in which there is no objectivity, and I see film as not being objective, and cinema verite as a cinema of lies that depends on the art of telling yourself lies. If you’re a good storyteller then the lie is more true than reality, and if you’re a bad one, the truth is worse than a half lie.
explicacao: Citação de Jean Rouch sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “I look at the human sciences as poetic sciences in which there is no objectivity, and I see film …” articula reflexao, cinema-verite, fiction em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como termino e fiction.
